sanskrit:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च।
पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः॥
transliteration: saṅkaro narakāyaiva kula-ghnānāṃ kulasya ca | patanti pitaro hy eṣāṃ lupta-piṇḍodaka-kriyāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Unwanted progeny drags the clan and its destroyers toward hell; the forefathers of such lineages fall because the offerings of food and water have ceased.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices a dread that moral collapse will sever the invisible lifeline between generations, leaving ancestors—and by extension the living self—adrift without identity or continuity. The verse exposes how the fear of cultural extinction can eclipse even the fear of death, turning duty into a desperate attempt to keep the inner and outer family system psychologically alive.
psychological_state: A person paralyzed by the conviction that personal failure will erase both ancestral memory and any future place in the human story.
trigger_scenario: A modern parent discovering their teenager has abandoned the family’s spiritual or cultural practices, triggering panic that the lineage and their own posthumous remembrance will vanish.
anchor_text: Protect the family line to keep ancestors—and your own identity—alive.
